message:
Fix anatomy mode menu breakage, soften audio, eliminate DecodingText flashing

Anatomy mode stacking context bug (lib/site-state.tsx):
  The .anatomy-mode CSS class applied filter: contrast(1.1)
  brightness(1.05) grayscale(0.1), which created a new CSS stacking
  context. This trapped all fixed-position children (site header,
  terminal overlay, mobile nav) inside the filtered container,
  preventing them from rendering above page content. Fix: removed
  the filter entirely — the grid overlay, scanlines, and green
  outlines already provide sufficient visual differentiation.

  Additional anatomy mode refinements:
  - Lowered grid overlay z-index from 9998 to 50 so it no longer
    covers interactive elements (buttons, links, inputs)
  - Subtler scanline: 15s period (was 10s), thinner opacity
  - Softer image/video desaturation: grayscale(0.4) (was 1.0)
  - Removed header from outline selector (visual noise)

Audio improvements:
  - Zap sound: 2000Hz sawtooth → 600Hz sine, gain 0.05 → 0.04,
    duration 200ms → 150ms. The original was an ear-piercing screech
  - Removed double sound on anatomy toggle: was firing both
    playSfx("zap") and playSfx("click"), now just "click"

DecodingText performance rewrite (components/decoding-text.tsx):
  Root cause: The old implementation used useState + setInterval to
  update displayed characters every ~17-89ms (depending on text
  length). Each setState triggered a full React re-render. With 3
  DecodingText instances inside a FrankenGlitch wrapper (which
  renders children 3x during glitch), this created ~60 re-renders/sec
  in the hero — thrashing the browser's compositor alongside the
  auto-playing video element and causing visible UI flashing.

  Fix: Replaced useState with useRef + direct DOM mutation
  (el.textContent = ...) — zero React re-renders during animation.
  Switched from setInterval to requestAnimationFrame for proper
  vsync alignment. Added invisible ghost span to reserve layout
  space and prevent CLS during the decode animation. The visual
  scrambling effect is identical but now entirely off React's
  reconciliation path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
